---
title: "Scale reliability and validity"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Scale reliability and validity}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(surveyframe)
```

Scale analysis starts with item membership stored in the instrument.

```{r validity}
loadings <- list(
  SAT = c(sat_1 = .72, sat_2 = .81, sat_3 = .77),
  TRUST = c(trust_1 = .75, trust_2 = .79, trust_3 = .73)
)
validity_report(loadings)
```

Reliability diagnostics use the optional `psych` package and are skipped when
it is not installed.

```{r reliability, eval = requireNamespace("psych", quietly = TRUE)}
agree5 <- sf_choices("agree5", 1:5,
  c("Strongly disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral", "Agree", "Strongly agree"))
i1 <- sf_item("sat_1", "Reliable", type = "likert", choice_set = "agree5", scale_id = "sat")
i2 <- sf_item("sat_2", "Responsive", type = "likert", choice_set = "agree5", scale_id = "sat")
i3 <- sf_item("sat_3", "Recommend", type = "likert", choice_set = "agree5", scale_id = "sat")
scale <- sf_scale("sat", "Satisfaction", c("sat_1", "sat_2", "sat_3"))
instr <- sf_instrument("Service Survey", components = list(agree5, i1, i2, i3, scale))
resp <- data.frame(sat_1 = c(4, 5, 3, 4), sat_2 = c(5, 4, 3, 4), sat_3 = c(4, 5, 2, 4))
reliability_report(resp, instr, omega = FALSE)
```
